jokes.text: - They closed the company. - For forgiving his debt. - Don't mind me, I work downstairs. - Hi. Doing a little lunch looting.
jokes.setup: Employee casually mentions taking food from the closed office
jokes.punchline: 'Lunch looting' - alliterative euphemism for theft
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that makes theft sound innocent and organized

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, a lot of people's first house was a Bluth. No, no, I was part of that Cornballer class action suit.
jokes.setup: Michael proudly mentions putting people in their first house
jokes.punchline: Turns out it was because of a lawsuit against the family's defective product
jokes.explanation: Perfect Arrested Development callback that recontextualizes Michael's pride into family shame

jokes.text: I made enough to put a down payment on a Sitwell. Well, we've put a lot of people in Sitwells, too.
jokes.setup: Employee mentions using lawsuit money to buy a competitor's house
jokes.punchline: Michael admits they've also hurt people with their competitor's products
jokes.explanation: Escalates the previous joke while referencing ongoing family rivalry

jokes.text: Uh... oh, God, well, you caught us. Didn't think anybody would be getting on while we were getting off.
jokes.setup: GOB explains being caught in elevator
jokes.punchline: Unintentional sexual innuendo with 'getting on while getting off'
jokes.explanation: Standard accidental innuendo, fits GOB's oblivious character

jokes.text: An iPhone 5 charger! Fourth floor.
jokes.setup: GOB proudly shows off the new office building
jokes.punchline: The big achievement is finding an outdated iPhone charger
jokes.explanation: Specific obsolete technology reference makes the company's desperation funnier

jokes.text: Just to see the roof of a Rite Aid? Which was the first that G.O.B. had heard of the view.
jokes.setup: Michael questions the expensive office move for a view
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals GOB didn't even know about the view he bragged about
jokes.explanation: Perfect narrator-reveal joke exposing GOB's incompetence

jokes.text: It used to be a 2 Hour Teeth, the whole place. Yeah, I remember the screams. I guess it was the 90 Minute Tooth guys across the street that put the 2 Hour guys out of business.
jokes.setup: Discussion of former dental office tenant
jokes.punchline: Absurd competitive dentistry with increasingly fast/horrifying service
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation of competitive dentistry with horrifying implications

jokes.text: I did see that the old Blockbuster is becoming an 'Hour Mouth,' so those guys gotta be shitting themselves.
jokes.setup: Continuation of fast dentistry competition
jokes.punchline: Even faster 'Hour Mouth' service causing panic
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation with vulgar punchline completing the absurd dentistry war

jokes.text: Boy, what's a bike tire doing on the roof of the Rite Aid? Yeah, that's the $14,000 a month question, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Michael spots random bike tire on roof
jokes.punchline: GOB's defensive response reveals the expensive rent is for this terrible view
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual gag that undermines GOB's expensive office move with absurd detail

jokes.text: You're the smart Bluth, I can see it in your eyes. You read, don't you?
jokes.setup: Adhir addresses Michael with what seems like a compliment
jokes.punchline: Reading is considered remarkable intelligence for a Bluth
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy that establishes how low the bar is for Bluth intelligence

jokes.text: Or as I like to call him, Chief Worrywart. / It's 'compliance officer.'
jokes.setup: GOB introduces Adhir's job title
jokes.punchline: GOB's childish nickname vs. Adhir's correction
jokes.explanation: Shows GOB's immaturity but not particularly original

jokes.text: That's it, go to your office, Adhir. He's the chief compliant officer, so he has to do whatever I comply.
jokes.setup: GOB dismisses Adhir and explains his role
jokes.punchline: Misunderstands 'compliance officer' as 'compliant officer' and misuses 'comply'
jokes.explanation: Good malapropism that shows GOB's business incompetence through wordplay

jokes.text: Like you, I'm about to go underwater, but before I go, I'd like to give you some advice: you're not good at this.
jokes.setup: Michael's metaphorical statement about going underwater
jokes.punchline: Brutally direct advice delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Michael's characteristic blunt honesty delivered with perfect timing

jokes.text: I will get a trophy for Dad, for... something. Figure that out later.
jokes.setup: GOB decides to get his father a trophy
jokes.punchline: Hasn't thought of what the trophy would be for
jokes.explanation: Classic GOB logic - grand gesture without any planning or meaning

jokes.text: Then why did you move into your son's dorm room? That was a year, okay? And not even a full year, because I got there at Thanksgiving.
jokes.setup: Michael calls out GOB's hypocrisy about father-son distance
jokes.punchline: GOB's defensive justification actually makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup-punchline that exposes GOB's lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: Not only are you finding me in your son's pants, but you're also finding out how the sausage is made.
jokes.setup: Michael discovers Tobias in George Michael's clothes
jokes.punchline: Tobias uses sexual metaphor while literally in another man's pants
jokes.explanation: Peak Tobias - unintentional sexual innuendo while in a compromising position

jokes.text: No one was buying me as a straight man. It was a little off-brand.
jokes.setup: Tobias explains his acting challenges
jokes.punchline: Completely oblivious double meaning about his sexuality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant double meaning that works on multiple levels with perfect character integration

jokes.text: Lindsay has... what does Gwyneth Paltrow say? 'Consciously un-cuckold' me.
jokes.setup: Tobias references Gwyneth Paltrow's 'conscious uncoupling'
jokes.punchline: Adapts it to his specific cuckold situation
jokes.explanation: Clever adaptation of celebrity speak to Tobias's humiliating situation

jokes.text: Michael, if I could... put my therapist leggings on for one second.
jokes.setup: Tobias wants to offer psychological advice
jokes.punchline: 'Therapist leggings' instead of hat - weird clothing metaphor
jokes.explanation: Odd Tobiasism that's funny but not as sharp as his best work

jokes.text: I suppose I did... start too high. Okay, let's bring everyone down an octave.
jokes.setup: Tobias's therapy role-play goes wrong
jokes.punchline: Treats emotional intensity like a musical performance
jokes.explanation: Good Tobias moment mixing his actor training with inappropriate situation

jokes.text: I wish I had known you'd had such depth before I gave your part away to a less experienced actor. I didn't know there was such a thing.
jokes.setup: Tobias regrets giving away Michael's part to someone less experienced
jokes.punchline: Michael's deadpan response that he didn't know less experienced actors existed
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man delivery that burns both Tobias and the replacement actor

jokes.text: He has one personality trait: he always comes back. Well, that and he always has his hands in his pockets. You got peanuts in there?
jokes.setup: Tobias describes George Michael's character traits
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific detail about hands in pockets, then asks about peanuts
jokes.explanation: References running visual gag about pockets, but delivery feels forced

jokes.text: No, we leave at 6:00 p.m. You telling me we've got a 14-bell delay?
jokes.setup: Captain explains departure time to confused Michael
jokes.punchline: Michael tries to sound nautical but uses wrong terminology
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from Michael trying to use nautical jargon incorrectly

jokes.text: Looking for a grandparent? No, thank you. I... No, I have enough.
jokes.setup: Employee asks if George Michael is looking for a grandparent
jokes.punchline: He politely declines as if it's a commodity
jokes.explanation: Funny absurdist moment treating grandparents like something you can have too many of

jokes.text: That's what the seniors do to keep the young people away. They don't want 'dot-commers' taking over.
jokes.setup: George Michael asks why the lobby is off-putting
jokes.punchline: Seniors deliberately make it unwelcoming to keep out tech workers
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about generational housing conflicts

jokes.text: The moment when a young woman realizes... Oh, my God, I've become my father. ♪ In the most delicious way! ♪
jokes.setup: Maeby describes seeing herself aged
jokes.punchline: References becoming her father with musical sting
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that ties Maeby's scam to broader family dysfunction

jokes.text: I didn't want to say anything, but I was like, 'Maeby looks like a 65-year-old.' What if I told you I was 75? Holy shit. You look fantastic.
jokes.setup: George Michael admits he thought she looked old
jokes.punchline: Age reveal completely changes his perspective
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke about relative aging expectations

jokes.text: When people catch on, they either forget or they die.
jokes.setup: George Michael asks how she gets away with the scam
jokes.punchline: Darkly pragmatic explanation about targeting seniors
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark Maeby logic delivered with casual cruelty

jokes.text: Lost all my personal paperwork in Hurricane Cantina.
jokes.setup: Maeby explains her missing paperwork
jokes.punchline: 'Hurricane Cantina' instead of Katrina - sounds like a restaurant
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but feels somewhat forced

jokes.text: Do you realize this condo is listed as Lucille Austero's? Yes. I am her. Sister. I am her sister.
jokes.setup: Stan challenges Maeby's presence
jokes.punchline: Maeby's awkward improvised excuse with obvious pauses
jokes.explanation: Classic improvised lie with visible thought process - realistic awkwardness

jokes.text: My silky pajamas, my peekaboo bras, my ticklers, sex knobs, and lacy hoo-has
jokes.setup: Maeby threatens to leave with her belongings
jokes.punchline: Increasingly inappropriate items for an 'elderly woman' to mention
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of inappropriate items, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: He's got horrible aids. Oh, hearing aids. Yeah, yeah, here they call them aids. The other ones they call aids.
jokes.setup: Maeby mentions Stan's hearing problems
jokes.punchline: Confusion between hearing aids and AIDS
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay that feels dated and relies on cheap confusion

jokes.text: Don't worry, if he asks who you are, I'll just tell him you're Tom Collins. He's always asking for a Tom Collins.
jokes.setup: Maeby offers to provide George Michael with fake identity
jokes.punchline: Reveals elderly man constantly requests cocktail, mistaking it for person
jokes.explanation: Clever elderly confusion joke - man repeatedly asking for drink thinking it's a person

jokes.text: You perverted that into a way to not hurt him.
jokes.setup: George Michael defends his lying about Rebel
jokes.punchline: Maeby accuses him of perverting her advice into kindness
jokes.explanation: Clever reversal where kindness is treated as the perversion

jokes.text: You passed out at the aquarium. They make you feel like you're underwater with all these giant... Dolphins? I think that was some kind of smiling, kind of bemused baby shark.
jokes.setup: Maeby explains George Michael's fear of underwater
jokes.punchline: His confused description of what scared him at the aquarium
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing George Michael's neurotic overanalysis

jokes.text: You tell her, 'I'm here for a quickie', and I'm out of here.' Mr. Brash. And she'll drop you like a buttered cane.
jokes.setup: Maeby gives George Michael dating advice
jokes.punchline: 'Drop you like a buttered cane' - nonsensical old person saying
jokes.explanation: Funny invented old person saying that sounds authentic but meaningless

jokes.text: My wife died there. Ovarian cancer, actually. He's a liar. And... He shouldn't have said that.
jokes.setup: Michael shares personal tragedy with strangers
jokes.punchline: Strangers' uncomfortable reaction to oversharing
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Michael's social awkwardness when processing grief

jokes.text: Lied to me, and that's worse.
jokes.setup: Michael discovers the family kept the cottage
jokes.punchline: Uses his son's signature phrase
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Michael has internalized his son's judgment

jokes.text: Were we supposed to lose our precious daughter-in-law and a couple hundred grand in the same year?
jokes.setup: Lucille defends keeping the cottage secret
jokes.punchline: Equates wife's death with financial loss
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille moment showing her complete lack of emotional awareness

jokes.text: That totally lovely Rita. Uh, she was a smart cookie, that one.
jokes.setup: Lucille catches herself about to insult Rita
jokes.punchline: Calls the mentally disabled Rita a 'smart cookie'
jokes.explanation: Good callback with characteristic Lucille obliviousness to irony

jokes.text: H-Honestly, I never thought you'd get past the guard gate.
jokes.setup: Michael asks if she was worried he'd find the cottage
jokes.punchline: Her casual admission of relying on security to keep him out
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille logic - casual cruelty delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Am I supposed to be me? No! You are... I'm... A girl. You're a girl, Linds, uh, Murphy B... Lucille.
jokes.setup: Confusion over who's playing which character
jokes.punchline: Tobias struggles to explain Lindsay should play a woman
jokes.explanation: Mild confusion humor but lacks the sharp writing of better scenes

jokes.text: It's basically Michael without the peanut pockets.
jokes.setup: Tobias describes Lucille 2's character
jokes.punchline: Reduces character to absence of Michael's trait
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier pockets joke with character analysis twist

jokes.text: Actually, sometimes, I think I want to be a dog doctor. Ow!
jokes.setup: Lindsay questions her acting commitment
jokes.punchline: Says 'dog doctor' instead of 'veterinarian', gets hit
jokes.explanation: Classic Lindsay simplistic language use - 'dog doctor' shows her vocabulary limitations

jokes.text: I know 50 gentlemen who would tuck their last nut to play this part.
jokes.setup: Tobias emphasizes the value of playing Lucille Austero
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate expression mixing desperation with body parts
jokes.explanation: Good Tobias inappropriateness with creative vulgar expression

jokes.text: Does this mean we can start leaving towels on the floor?
jokes.setup: Buster emerges after family secrets are revealed
jokes.punchline: His first concern is about towel rules
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster moment showing his childish priorities amid family drama

jokes.text: I'm making this into a trophy for Dad. / I can't wait to see the one you got yourself.
jokes.setup: Buster is crafting something for their father
jokes.punchline: Michael's sarcastic assumption that Buster got himself a trophy
jokes.explanation: Michael's sarcasm reveals family dynamics but not particularly original

jokes.text: It's a good time to be in this business. Everyone gets a trophy. I tell people to stay away from the business. It's always been a lousy business, and these days everybody shops online. Everybody gets a trophy.
jokes.setup: Trophy store owner discusses his business
jokes.punchline: Contradicts himself about whether it's good or bad business
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about participation trophy culture

jokes.text: So the guy was like, 'Are you shitting me?' That's usually the sign that you've made yourself a great deal.
jokes.setup: GOB describes buying the trophy store
jokes.punchline: Misinterprets shocked disbelief as approval
jokes.explanation: Good GOB logic showing his complete misreading of social situations

jokes.text: No, he didn't even have keys to the back room. Also, the heat doesn't turn off.
jokes.setup: GOB explains trophy store problems
jokes.punchline: Reveals he bought a store without full access or working climate control
jokes.explanation: Continues GOB's pattern of terrible business decisions with specific details

jokes.text: At least he did something that we have not been able to do. He stood up for himself when his father was wrong.
jokes.setup: Michael defends his son punching him
jokes.punchline: Admits his son was right to punch him
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Michael's growth and self-awareness

jokes.text: I told her, 'He's gonna take us from the beach.'
jokes.setup: George Sr. explains his prediction about Michael
jokes.punchline: He predicted Michael would find them via the beach
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from George Sr.'s oddly specific prediction

jokes.text: Stacy was the paralegal I used to bring out there who went into insulin shock at the very height of the market.
jokes.setup: George Sr. corrects himself about who died at the cottage
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of another death with financial timing concern
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing George Sr.'s callousness and poor timing of tragedies

jokes.text: You know, I smashed my phone a couple years ago, too. With a hammer. Never missed it. Miss the hammer, but I guess that's why. God made rocks.
jokes.setup: Dusty mentions smashing his phone
jokes.punchline: Misses the hammer more than the phone, then explains rocks as replacements
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic from beach hippie character

jokes.text: Van Halen was in town this weekend rocking audiences. But some beachgoers are getting rocked in a different way... Nirvana was in town... 50 Cent was in town...
jokes.setup: News reports use same format across decades
jokes.punchline: 'Rocked' pun continues through different musical eras
jokes.explanation: Decent running gag showing lazy journalism across decades

jokes.text: Get off my beach, you stupid seagulls.
jokes.setup: Dusty is protective of the beach
jokes.punchline: Yells at seagulls as if they're human intruders
jokes.explanation: Establishes Dusty's territorial nature extends beyond humans

jokes.text: Care to join me for a glass of breakfast wine?
jokes.setup: Lucille offers Dusty a drink
jokes.punchline: Normalizes drinking wine at breakfast
jokes.explanation: Classic Lucille moment treating alcoholism as sophistication

jokes.text: I don't know, maybe she did not hear 'I'm leaving' when I said, uh, 'I ain't going nowhere.'
jokes.setup: Michael explains his relationship confusion
jokes.punchline: Realizes his double negative meant the opposite of what he intended
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay joke showing Michael's communication failures
